cards.back: If every non-empty subset of a set that is well-ordered has a least element, then the set itself is also well-ordered.
A method of proof that uses two steps: the base case and the inductive step.
The first step in mathematical induction where the property is proven for the smallest possible value.
The second step in mathematical induction where the property is proven for all other values, assuming it's true for the smallest value.
A set in which every non-empty subset has a least element.
